{{参照方法|date=11 September 2019}}
{{nihongo|'''Minoshiri no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|三野後国造|''mino no michinoshiri no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''mino no michinoshiri no kokuzō''}} was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled the eastern part of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}.

== Overview ==
=== Name ===
Recorded as '''Minoshiri no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}''.

=== Ancestry ===
* According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', {{ill|Omikafura-no-Mikoto|ja|臣賀夫良命}}, a grandson of {{ill|Izumo-no-Ō-Omi-no-Mikoto|ja|出雲大臣命}}, was appointed Minoshiri no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|lt=Emperor Seimu|lt_ja=成務朝}}.

=== Clan ===
The '''{{ill|Minoshiri clan|ja|美濃後氏}}''' (''mino no shiri-uji''; {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Atai (kabane)|ja|直 (姓)|lt=Atai|lt_ja=直}}). Of the same line as the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}; its kin include the {{ill|Tōtsuōmi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|遠淡海国造}}, {{ill|Kunu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|久努国造}}, {{ill|Suruga no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|珠流河国造}}, {{ill|Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|久自国造}}, {{ill|Ochi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造}}, and {{ill|Inaba no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|稲葉国造}}.

== Base ==
The kuni no miyatsuko's base is thought to have been centered on '''{{ill|Mount Inaba|ja|金華山 (岐阜県)|lt=Mount Inaba|lt_ja=稲葉山}}'''. At the foot of Mount Inaba is the {{ill|Engishiki shrine|ja|式内社}} {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}, which also shares its altar with the shikinaisha Mononobe Shrine.

=== Territory ===
The kuni no miyatsuko's territory was the region then known as '''Minoshiri Province''' — corresponding to the later '''{{ill|Kakami District|ja|各務郡}}''', '''{{ill|Kata District|ja|方県郡}}''', and '''{{ill|Kani District|ja|可児郡}}''' of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}, present-day {{ill|Kakamigahara, Gifu|ja|各務原市|lt=Kakamigahara City|lt_ja=各務原市}}, {{ill|Gifu (city)|ja|岐阜市|lt=Gifu City|lt_ja=岐阜市}}, and {{ill|Kani, Gifu|ja|可児市|lt=Kani City|lt_ja=可児市}} in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. {{ill|Yōrō District|ja|養老郡}} may also have been related.<ref>Hōga Toshio, ''Kodai Shizoku no Kenkyū 9: The Kibi Clan — A Great Regional Lineage with the Momotarō Legend'', Aogaki Shuppan, 2016.</ref>

== Tutelary Shrine ==
Presumably {{ill|Inaba Shrine|ja|伊奈波神社}}, the '''{{ill|Sannomiya|ja|三宮}}''' (third shrine) of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}.

=== Tomb ===
Kafura-no-Mikoto Tomb — within the precincts of Kogane Shrine, Kane-machi, Gifu City; designated by Gifu City.

== Notes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

== References ==
{{No footnotes|section=1|date=11 September 2019}}
{{ページ番号|section=1|date=11 September 2019}}
*''Kokushi Daijiten'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}).
*Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Ujizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan).
*''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan).
*''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ({{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}).
*''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoten).
*''Nihonshi Sōran'' ({{ill|Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}).

== See also ==
*{{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:みののしりのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:美濃国]]
[[Category:美濃国の氏族]]
[[Category:物部氏|-みののしりのくにのみやつこ]]
[[Category:岐阜県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:三野後国造]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
